intro: I figure it out and I build stuff.
summary: Some of it is practical, some of it is experimental, and some of it starts as an excuse to explore something unfamiliar. What stays consistent is how I work: break the problem down, keep the solution simple, and keep pushing until it holds up. I like experimentation, but I trust simple ideas, clear systems, and software that feels understandable long after the first version ships
principles: Break problems down until they move.
Build things that are actually useful.
notes: I enjoy work that sits somewhere between systems thinking and product sense: software that is technically solid but still built for real people and real use.
A lot of my strongest work is backend-heavy, but I like being close enough to the product to understand what the system is actually trying to enable.
Outside the more serious engineering work, I also like building smaller experimental things simply because they are fun to make and teach a different kind of problem-solving.
